prompt:
Escalation from the fulfillment team — two complaints that they swear started together, and both reproduce on a clean checkout: `go test -race -timeout 120s ./...` covers them.

1. Stock listings go strange right after someone opens the availability dashboard. GET /warehouses/{id}/stock normally comes back ordered by SKU; the moment anyone pulls that warehouse's report, the listing comes back in a different order — and the next restock of a SKU we already carry shows up as a SECOND row for that SKU instead of topping up the existing one, so the on-hand count is split across two rows from then on. Reconciliation then flags mismatches on that warehouse even though every single movement went through the API; nothing happened off the books.

2. Any night the floor team runs a counting session (lock, count, unlock), the reconciliation ledger goes bad afterwards. The job correctly waits out the count and retries a little later — but from the next night on there are TWO ledger entries per night, roughly fifteen minutes apart, and it never settles back to one per night.

Please work out what's actually happening and fix it. stockroom_test.go is the acceptance bar and is read-only.
